summary: Context Menu for Multiple Files (only remove links)

description.text: Contributes to #12567 ### Description This pull request addresses the issue where, on a bibtex entry that contains multiple linked files, only single file actions can be done to the linked files. For actions like “Remove link”, files must be removed one at a time. Our change redefines the implementation of the context menu relating to linked files, allowing for actions to be triggered on a multi-file selection. When a selection on multiple linked files is made, a unique context menu appears with valid actions that can be performed on multiple files at once. ### Change Locations * main/…/gui/fieldeditors/contextmenu/ContextMenuFactory.java > Refactored context menu creation logic into new factory class to cleanly separate concerns and make the context menu logic to test in isolation. * main/…/gui/fieldeditors/contextmenu/ContextAction.java > Moved into an independent class so the ContextAction object can be reused across LinkedFilesEditor class and ContextMenuFactoryTest class. * main/…/gui/fieldeditors/contextmenu/MultiContextAction.java > Defined MultiContextAction class to handle actions pertaining to multi-file selections. > > Allows for reuse like with the ContextAction class. * main/…/gui/fieldeditors/LinkedFilesEditor.java > Detached context menu instantiation from ViewModelListCellFactory’s “.withContextMenu”. Context menu lifecycle is now handled by the mouse click event handler. This is due to an issue occurring with the incorrect context menu appearing on a multi-selection when right-clicking the first item versus right-clicking on the other highlighted items (we believe this pertains to something happening with javaFX). > > Modified LinkedFilesEditor class to delegate context menu creation to the ContextMenuFactory class, removing tightly coupled logic and replacing it with injected behaviour for better maintainability and testability. ### Next Steps Due to time constraints, we have implemented the “Remove link” action to work with multi-file selections at this time, however, we believe our refactoring of the context menu creation process allows for easier implementation of the other context actions to work with multi-file selections. ### Tests * test/…/gui/fieldeditors/contextmenu/ContextMenuFactoryTest.java Defined new test suite that covers creation of single and multi-file selection context menus, verifying correct invoking of action handlers, empty selections and null-safe label matching. ### Screenshots * Selecting and removing link for single file * Selecting and removing link for multiple selected files ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [/] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I outlined it in this pull request. - [x] [Checked documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I created an issue at or, even better, I submitted a pull request to the documentation repository.

<!-- LINK THE ISSUE WITH THE "Closes" KEYWORD -->
<!-- Example: Closes (link) OR Closes #12345 -->

Closes #12843 

This pull request addresses an issue where the dialog prompting users about including cross-references reappears even after the user has selected the "Do not ask again" option. Additionally, it resolves the problem of the updated state of preferences not being saved correctly, ensuring that the last saved state persists across sessions.

* Use EasyBind.listen() in `getCopyToPreferences` to observe any change in these properties to save the preferences.
* Implemented property binding in  `CopyToPreferences` class.

_Screen Recordings:_ 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79ee2755-3576-42ad-8284-516a07f7c04e

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/88e5359e-c797-462d-926d-7722d642b9d4

summary: PostgreSQL processes remain running after JabRef crashes or is forcefully terminated

description.text:
### JabRef version

Latest development branch build (please note build date below)

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

Windows 10

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

When JabRef crashes or is forcefully terminated (e.g., via `kill -9` or Task Manager), associated PostgreSQL processes often remain running. These orphaned processes continue to consume system resources unnecessarily.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Start JabRef with a PostgreSQL backend.
2. Forcefully terminate JabRef (e.g., using `kill -9` or Task Manager).
3. Open Task Manager (Windows) or use `ps` on Linux/macOS.
4. Observe that multiple `PostgreSQL Server` processes are still running.

### Expected Behavior

All PostgreSQL-related processes spawned by JabRef should terminate when JabRef is unexpectedly closed.

### Actual Behavior

PostgreSQL processes persist after JabRef exits unexpectedly, leading to orphaned background processes.


### Appendix

### Screenshot

![PostgreSQL orphaned processes](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/825ef40a-05e4-4ff9-9247-5c9d80cd37e3)

> Screenshot shows several idle PostgreSQL Server processes still running after JabRef was terminated.

### Sub-issue 1:
1. Start JabRef
2. Open a library X (and save it)
3. Open another library Y (and save it)
4. Have (one or more) entries in the library X with cross-references (and save). Example: 

    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e3b08fd-693a-4d3f-8276-a2b4c86be286)
4. Right click the entry/entries with cross references.
5. Select "Copy to" -> "Y"
6. Tick "Do not ask again" and then select "Include":
      
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/23499016-0f08-4d3e-be25-5cc627d1a0e0)
7. Restart JabRef
8. Open the two saved libraries X and Y (if not open already)
9. Repeat steps 5-6.
10. You will observe that the dialog (in step 7) appears again even though we ticked "Do not ask again". (Expected: It respects last saved preferences, i.e., does not appear).

#### Related PRs:
- https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12374
- https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12503

### Sub-issue 2:
1. Start JabRef
2. Go to File -> Preferences
3. In "General" -> "User Interface", observe the state of the option "Ask whether to include cross-references when copying to another library".
    
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/185c2c9a-4888-4e89-a378-f609dac924bd)

4. Change its state (in this case, it is checked, so we un-check it)
5. Click 'Save"
6. Restart JabRef
7. Repeat steps 1-3. You will find that the box (in step 3) is checked again (updated state of preferences was not saved). (Expected: Last saved state of the preference persists across sessions).

#### Related PR:
- https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12526

### Test for completion (for both sub-issues)
1. The state of the preferences are saved across sessions. 
2. (After 1 is ensured) - Double-check that the functionality (not just saved state) of the respective options works as expected too after the restart.

Screen recordings are preferable for easy verification.

summary: Fix : Importing .bib file shows correct message and imports groups associated

description.text:
Closes #11025 

**Problem:**
Previously, when importing a bib file without using the specific filter, this is, using "Any File", the popup message said "Importing file _file-name_ as unknown format". It would also not import the groups from the imported file to the library.

This was caused by a method call in `org/jabref/gui/importer/ImportCommand.java` trying to convert the filter "Any File" to an importer, which would cause a `null` value to be returned, as there is no importer for the filter "Any File".

![PR](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2fd67ab3-4100-4b27-b33f-e316c48049e1)

Then, when the code would try to verify if the importer was empty or not, it entered the "unknown format" block, since the previous operation returned a `null` value. This would also cause the groups to not be imported.

![PR 2](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ad4de1a2-4d79-41ef-b456-335eee1ad683)

What was happening before:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79509ac9-870b-4819-b82e-9f1892fef1dd

**Solution:**
Before converting the filter "Any File" to an importer, we check the extension of the file being imported to see if it is a bib file or not. If it is, we convert the filter to BibTeX, so that there is a match to the specific importer. 
This way, the code enters the correct block, importing the groups as well, since the importer would not be empty anymore.


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e71bfaf8-0c5c-47d8-85cd-e7e7e861403f

### Mandatory checks

Go through the checklist below. It is mandatory, even for a draft pull request.

summary: Moved Journal Abbreviation from Quality Menu to Cleanup Entries Dialog

description.text:
<!-- YOU HAVE TO MODIFY THE TEXT BELOW TO FIT YOUR PR. OTHERWISE, YOUR PR WILL BE CLOSED WITHOUT FURTHER COMMENT. -->

Closes #11791 
This change moves the Journal Abbreviation behavior from the Quality Menu into the Cleanup Entries Dialog as follows:

The JabRef documentation will need to be updated. A new issue has been created to do this [here](https://github.com/JabRef/user-documentation/issues/559)

Before Changes
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e40199da-78d9-49cc-99ff-5ea675164437)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/637b6d93-9810-478a-8342-b0a42104a59b)

After Changes
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/355746ac-886d-4f85-a0f8-023e32c3c392)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3249583c-4a3d-4312-99a4-787320dafcbf)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7c80a3ae-d78c-436a-9422-b1a4e586d75f)

## Summary

This pull request implements the steps outlined in [this issue refinement](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11791#issuecomment-2423767374)

Specifically, all journal‐abbreviation tasks (both abbreviate and unabbreviate) from separate menu commands have been moved into the “Clean up entries” dialog. This allows the user to pick how they want to handle journal names—no change, abbreviate, or unabbreviate—directly inside the Cleanup dialog rather than from separate menu items.

**Key changes**:
- **Journal‐related** combo box in the Cleanup dialog. You can choose:
  1. **No changes (default)**
  2. **Abbreviate (default)**
  3. **Abbreviate (dotless)**
  4. **Abbreviate (shortest unique)**
  5. **Unabbreviate**
- Added two new cleanup classes:
  - **AbbreviateJournalCleanup** (replaces old *UndoableAbbreviator*)
  - **UnabbreviateJournalCleanup** (replaces old *UndoableUnabbreviator*)
- Removed the old "Abbreviate" and "Unabbreviate" items from the **Quality** menu, as well as old references in the code to *UndoableAbbreviator*, *UndoableUnabbreviator*, and the old keybindings for them.
- Added tests in `AbbreviateJournalCleanupTest` and `UnabbreviateJournalCleanupTest`, along with changes to `JournalAbbreviationRepositoryTest` so the coverage is the same as before but uses the new cleanup approach.

## Notable Design Details

To perform this change, the functionality in `UndoableAbbreviator` (and `UndoableUnabbreviator`) had to be adapted to the `org.jabref.logic.cleanup.CleanupJob` interface. I discovered while figuring out an implementation that `org.jabref.logic.cleanup.AbbreviateJournalCleanup` (and `org.jabref.logic.cleanup.AbbreviateJournalCleanup`) both needed access to a `JournalAbbreviationRepository` object. Many of the changes in this PR are related to this dependency being passed down from `org.jabref.gui.frame.MainMenu` and `org.jabref.gui.frame.MainToolBar`. This issue is further described at [this comment](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11791#issuecomment-2741338211) and [this comment](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11791#issuecomment-2745033765)

It was suggested [here](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11791#issuecomment-2737450413) that the journal abbreviation menu items instead be added to the field formatters in the UI, and implemented as a Formatter. The reason this was **not** done is because all the formatters act as stateless transformations, and are instantiated without any parameters (see `org.jabref.logic.formatter.Formatters`). Journal Abbreviation is notably not stateless, as it needs access to `JournalAbbreviationRepository`. Theoretically, this could be overcome with dependency injection into the logic layer, but I believe that violates the architecture.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bb3243b3-3916-48f4-b2c5-6afa3c96e4bb)


## How To Test

**IMPORTANT NOTE**: When testing and switching from running JabRef on this branch to running JabRef on the main branch, I had an unhandled exception because a new enum constant (e.g. `UNABBREVIATE`) persisted in the user preferences, but the code on main doesn't define this constant:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e590c1be-132d-451b-bc97-d6145870374b)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e14370e0-004e-4457-8be3-a5a07b7cee3b)

The only workaround I have found for this is to go in JabRef to **File -> Preferences -> Reset Preferences** and then hit save.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/562065e7-465e-4c76-9838-1e611c2c6b38)


1. **Open the “Clean up entries” dialog** (Quality → “Clean up entries”).
2. Select a group of entries with different journal names.
3. In “Journal‐related,” pick **Abbreviate (default)** or **Unabbreviate**, then apply cleanup.
4. Observe that the journal fields have become either abbreviated or unabbreviated accordingly.
5. If you open the Cleanup dialog again, your choice in the combo box should persist in subsequent sessions (provided you store your preferences).

Additionally:
- Try “Abbreviate (dotless)” or “Abbreviate (shortest unique)” on a few entries with known abbreviations (like “Physical Review Letters” → “PRL”). 
- Run the tests at `org.jabref.logic.cleanup.AbbreviateJournalCleanupTest`, `org.jabref.logic.cleanup.UnabbreviateJournalCleanupTest`, and `org.jabref.logic.journals.JournalAbbreviationRepositoryTest`

### Mandatory checks
